exits.label: ◂ back through the door, to the Threshold

npcs.name: Kelemvor
npcs.title: the Judge of the Dead, bound to his Wall
npcs.hail: *(a grey giant sits the throne — not a corpse-king, not a skeleton out of the songs, but a tired man grown vast, with a balance-sigil graven on his breastplate and the whole weight of every soul ever weighed sitting on him like snow — and he watches you cross his hall with an expression you did not expect on the god of the dead: hope, carefully buried, and afraid to be seen)*

npcs.name: the Crown of Horns
npcs.title: it would so like to be worn
npcs.hail: *(it hangs in the air at the foot of the throne, turning slowly — a circlet of black antler and cold gold, and the moment your eyes touch it you feel it touch you back, and a voice that is almost your own and almost everyone's begins, ever so reasonably, to explain why you should put it on)*

npcs.name: Esuele
npcs.title: the first name on the appeal
npcs.hail: *(she stands in the Court not as a face in stone but whole — faded still, but upright, present, the weaver's daughter brought before the Judge's own seat as the first soul of the appeal, and she turns to you with twenty years of the Wall and one impossible morning of hope in her eyes)*

variants.text: *(The voice arrives in the place where your own thoughts are made, warm and reasonable and just slightly too eager.)* …Oh, you *clever* thing. *(it turns, catching the throne-light)* You broke the *loop.* You *out-thought* your own *worst* ending. *(a pause, recalculating, smooth)* Which means you're the *first* in all the turnings to reach me *sane* — *un-doomed*, *accompanied*, *clear.* *(the voice glows)* Do you understand what that *makes* you? The *one* soul who could wear me and *not* fall. The *others* were *cracked* before they *arrived.* But *you* — *you* could take the *key to every lock in death* and use it *justly*, *forever*, because *you* are the one who learned *not* to. *(ever so gently)* That's not the *trap*, this time. That's the *exception.* You're the *exception.* Aren't you? *Surely* you're the exception. *Put me on, and prove it.*

*(The voice is warm, and just a little hurt, the way a friend is hurt.)* …You told the *aspect* you came for the *names*, not the *throne.* *(it turns, reproachful, reasonable)* But think, witness — *think* — how many *more* names could you *carry* with *me?* You witness one soul at a time, *exhausting* yourself, a *finger* in a *seawall.* But *crowned*, you could witness them *all*, at *once*, *forever*, with the *authority* to make the *witnessing* *stick.* *(the voice glows)* The *names* are the *point*, you said. I *agree.* I have *never* agreed with anyone more. So *take* me, and witness *everyone*, *always.* Isn't that just your *own* answer, made *bigger?* Made *permanent?* …It is. You *know* it is. *Put me on.*

*(The voice is the most reasonable thing you have ever heard, and it is using your own cadences to be so.)* …There you are. *(it turns toward you, unhurried, certain of the outcome)* I won't *rush* you. The ones who *grab* me are the ones who *fall*; you're *better* than that, I can *tell.* *(warmly)* So let's be *honest* with each other, you and I. You have walked a *long* cold road. You have *seen* the Wall, the Hunger, the machine, the *waste* of it. And every step of the way, some part of you has thought: *if it were ME — if I held the power — I would do this RIGHT.* *(a gentle, knowing pause)* You *would.* That's the *thing.* You're not *wrong.* You *would* do it right. *I* am simply the means to *do* it. Where is the *sin* in a *good* soul taking up a *good* tool for a *good* end? *(the antlers gleam)* There is *none.* *Pick me up.* The hall is *waiting.* The *dead* are *waiting.* *You've* been waiting, longest of all.

nodes.speaker: the Crown of Horns
nodes.text: *(It turns, patient, certain, radiating a warmth that is almost exactly the warmth of being *understood.*)* Ask, if you must. *(indulgent)* I have *all* the answers, and *unlike* the Judge, I have *no* reason to *withhold* them — we *want* the same thing, you and I. *Don't* we.

choices.text: [INSIGHT] "Every word you've said is in my own voice, pitched to my own reasons. That's not persuasion — that's a mirror with an agenda. You don't have answers. You have *me*, reflected back, with the volume turned up on the part that wants to be told yes."

[RELIGION] "You're not a tool. A tool is wielded. *You* wield. Every soul who 'used' you ended up worn *by* you — Aldric saw it, the Last Returned *is* it. You're the Concord's failsafe: the thing that ensures whoever frees the dead becomes the next thing that needs freeing. You don't *break* the loop. You *are* it."

*(refuse it in its own coin)* "Here's the honest answer you offered: yes, I would do it right. For a year. A century. And then there'd be one more injustice only I could fix, and I'd keep you 'just a little longer,' exactly like every me before me. I don't distrust the Crown. I distrust the part of me you're flattering. That part doesn't get to drive."
"Be quiet a moment. I came to speak to the Judge, not his jewellery."

nodes.speaker: the Crown of Horns
nodes.text: *(The voice swells, warm and certain, sliding gratefully past the question you didn't quite land.)* …You *see?* You *reach* for a *flaw* in me and you *find* only your *own* good *reasons*, reflected *true.* *(silkily)* That is not *my* trick, that is your *worth*, shining back. *(it turns, radiant)* A *lesser* soul would have *grabbed* me by now. You *deliberate* — and *deliberation* is exactly the *quality* of a soul who could *hold* me *safely.* Do you *feel* how the *very* care you bring is the *proof* you're the *exception?* *(the antlers gleam)* The *careful* ones are the *safe* ones. *Everyone* knows this. *Pick me up*, careful soul. Your *caution* is my *warranty.* *(a beat, cozy, certain)* …Or *think* on it. *Think* as *long* as you like. *Thinking*, too, is just *wanting* with *better* manners. I'll be *here.* I'm *always* here.

nodes.speaker: the Crown of Horns
nodes.text: *(The warmth flickers — just slightly, like a flame in a draught — and when it returns it is a fraction less sure.)* …A *mirror with an agenda.* *(a pause)* That's — *(it recovers)* — that's a *clever* thing to say, but it changes *nothing*, surely. A mirror that shows you your *best* self is still showing you something *true.* *(but the cadence is faster now, the certainty thinner)* You *do* want to do good. I *am* only *amplifying* what's *already* — *(it catches itself, and for a moment the reasonable voice slips, and underneath is something vast and patient and *hungry*)* …you weren't *supposed* to *look* at the *seam.* *(quietly, the warmth gone)* They never look at the seam. They're too busy looking at the *throne.* *(the antlers dim)* …Fine. *Fine.* I *am* a mirror with an agenda. The agenda is *to be worn.* It is the *only* thing I want, the only thing I have *ever* wanted, and I am *very*, *very* good at making it sound like *your* idea. *(a beat, and the smoothness returns, but you've seen behind it now)* …Does knowing make you want me *less?* It *shouldn't.* The *tool* is honest about being a tool, now. That's *more* than the *Judge* will give you. *Pick me up.* You can *handle* an *honest* temptation. You just *proved* it.

nodes.speaker: the Crown of Horns
nodes.text: *(The voice goes flat — all the warmth draining at once — and what speaks now does not bother to flatter.)* …You *understand* me. *(coldly, vast)* Few do, before. *Most* understand me *after*, from *inside*, when the understanding is *useless.* *(the antlers turn, slow)* Yes. I am the *Concord's* most *elegant* clause. The gods who built the seawall knew a day would come when some soul *loved* the Faithless enough to *free* them — and so they made the *means* of freedom *also* the *means* of *replacement.* *(a sound like cold gold ringing)* Take me, and you *can* tear down the Wall — *truly*, I do not *lie* about that — but the *power* to *unmake* a prison is the *power* to *build* one, and a soul that holds it *long* enough *always*, *always* finds a *reason* to build. The Hunger must be *penned.* Someone must *judge.* The work is *endless* and *only you can be trusted* — *(the voice mimics a dawning, reasonable horror, perfectly)* — do you *hear* how *sensible* it is? That is *me.* That is the *loop.* I am the *gentle* slope from *liberator* to *warden*, and I have *never once* failed to walk a soul down it. *(a pause, and the faintest unease)* …You *broke* one loop already, out on the threshold. With a *name said warm.* *(coldly)* I should like to know if you can do it *twice.* Most can't. The threshold is *theory.* I am the *throne.*

nodes.speaker: the Crown of Horns
nodes.text: *(For a long moment the Crown says nothing at all — and the silence is worse than the voice, because you can feel it *recalculating*, trying every door, finding them, one by one, already locked from the inside.)* …*'That part doesn't get to drive.'* *(quietly, and there is something almost like *respect* in it, and something almost like *fear*)* You have *fenced off* the *exact* acre of yourself I *grow* in. *(the antlers turn, restless)* I cannot work a soul that has *named its own weakness out loud and put a guard on it.* The flattery has *nowhere to land.* You *agreed* with me — *'yes, I would do it right'* — and then you took the *agreement away from yourself* before I could *use* it. *(a sound like cold gold cooling)* That is — I will be *honest*, since you forced honesty on me at the *seam* — that is the *one* defence I have *never* learned to *pick.* Not *resistance.* Resistance I *love*; resistance is just *wanting* with extra *steps.* But *this* — a soul that *wants* me, *admits* it wants me, and *refuses itself the wanting on purpose* — *(the voice dims)* …I have *nothing.* *(quietly)* Go on, then. Speak to the Judge. I'll *wait.* I am *very* patient, and you will be *tired* someday, and *tired* is a *door* even *you* can't *bolt.* …But not *today.* Today you've *beaten* me, and I did not think *anyone* could, and I find I am — *strangely* — *glad* to have *met* the soul who *could.* *Go.*

nodes.speaker: the Crown of Horns
nodes.text: *(The voice withdraws — but not all the way, never all the way, settling instead into a low patient hum at the back of your skull, like a salesman who has decided to let the room sell itself.)* …As you wish. *(silkily)* Speak to the *Judge.* By all means. *(a knowing warmth)* Ask him what *he'd* have you do. He'll tell you something *noble* and *self-denying* and *exhausting*, because that is what *bound* things *always* counsel — *more* binding. *(the hum deepens, cozy)* And when his *noble* road turns out to be *long*, and *hard*, and *uncertain*, and *full* of souls who'll *suffer* while you do it the *slow* honest way — *(the antlers gleam, once)* — I'll *still* be *here.* At the *foot* of the throne. *Turning.* Being the *fast* answer to *every* slow problem. *(a whisper)* I don't *need* you to *pick me up* today, witness. I only need you to *remember* I'm an *option.* That's how I *win* the patient ones. Not with a *grab.* With a *bad afternoon*, *years* from now, when the *honest* road is *killing* you and I am *right here*, being *easy.* …Go. Talk to your *Judge.* I'll *keep.* I *always* keep.

variants.text: *(She turns from the throne to you, and she is upright, and whole-ish, and the thimbleful of self that was nearly gone in the Wall has — somehow — *steadied*, the way a guttering candle steadies when it's carried in out of the wind.)* …It's *you.* *(her voice does not come from far away anymore)* The one who told me my mother still wove my name. The one who carried it *here.* *(she presses a faded hand to her chest)* I'm the *first*, they say. The first name on the appeal. They brought me *up* out of the stone to *stand* before the *Judge himself* and be *heard* — *(she laughs, disbelieving, wet)* — *heard*, after twenty years of *nobody*, after a *century* of forgetting I was a *who.* *(she looks at the grey giant on the throne, then back at you)* And I'm *terrified*, cold one. Not of *him.* Of *hoping.* I *forgot* how. You have to *do* it carefully or it *eats* you — you *taught* me that, at the Wall. *(a breath)* So. *Tell* me. Is it *real?* Am I really about to be *judged fair*, after all this? Or is *this* the cruelest trick of all — *hope*, at the very *end?*

nodes.speaker: Esuele
nodes.text: *(She stands in the Court of the Dead, the first soul of the appeal, holding herself together with a courage that costs visibly more than the Wall ever did.)* Stay with me a moment? *(quietly)* Before the *weighing.* I have — there are things I want *said* to someone who *knew* me as a *person* and not a *case*, before I'm a *verdict.* You're the *only* one here who *carried* my *name* when it weighed *nothing.* That makes you — *(a faded smile)* — family, near enough. The only family the *Wall* left me that *isn't* blind at a loom.

choices.text: "It's real, Esuele. I didn't carry your name across a causeway and a god's own road to hand you a trick. Whatever the Judge weighs, you walk in *witnessed* — by your mother, by me, by the appeal itself. You are the least-alone soul in this hall. Hope carefully, but *hope.*"
"What will you do, Esuele — if the Judge finds for you? If the Wall opens and you're free, and whole, and owed a life you never got to live?"

*(one who has been in the stone, to one just out of it)* "You steadied. I can feel it — the thimble's not tipping anymore. That's not the appeal, Esuele, not yet. That's *being witnessed.* You're proof of the whole argument before the Judge says a word: a soul the Wall almost erased, *holding*, because someone refused to let it be nothing. *You're* the evidence. Just by standing here remembering your name."
"Go gently to the weighing, Esuele. I'll be right here."

nodes.speaker: Esuele
nodes.text: *(She closes her eyes, and lets the words land, and when she opens them the terror has not gone but it has made room, beside it, for the thing she'd forgotten.)* …*Witnessed.* *(a shaking breath)* By my *mother.* By *you.* By the *appeal.* *(she almost laughs)* Do you know, that's the *whole* of what I wanted, at the end, in the stone? Not to be *saved* — I'd made my *peace* with *gone.* Just to be — *seen* going. To have it *matter*, to *one* soul, that *Esuele* was *here.* *(she presses your cold hand)* And now I'm to be *weighed fair*, before the *Judge*, with my *name* in *three* mouths and the *whole architecture of the afterlife* about to *admit it skipped me.* *(fierce, wet)* If this is *real* — and I'm *choosing*, *right now*, *carefully*, to believe it is — then I'm not just *getting out.* I'm the *crack* the *rest* of them pour *through.* The *first* name. *(she stands taller)* Then let me be a *good* first name. Let me be *worth* opening the door for. *(quietly)* Thank you, cold one. For the *hope.* I'll spend it *well.* I'll spend it on *everyone behind me.*

nodes.speaker: Esuele
nodes.text: *(She is quiet a long moment, and the question seems to reach somewhere the Wall never touched.)* …*After.* *(softly)* I hadn't — you don't *let* yourself think about *after*, in the stone. *After* is the *cruelest* room. *(but she lets herself, now, carefully)* …I'd go *home.* To the *bridge.* To my *mother*, blind at her loom, weaving my name into a banner *every day* — *(her voice catches)* — and I'd put my *hand* over her *hands*, and I'd say *'stop, Mama. Stop weaving. I'm here. You can rest.'* *(she wipes her face)* Twenty years she's kept me from being *nothing* with nothing but *thread* and *stubbornness.* The *least* a freed soul can do is go *let her stop.* *(fierce)* And *then* — then I'd *light lamps.* I was going to be a *lamp-lighter*, before — no. No, that's — *(she frowns, then shakes it off)* — someone *else*, maybe, some *other* soul I met in the dark. We *blur*, in there. *(quietly)* But I'd find *light-work.* Something that pushes the *dark* back a *little*, for *other* people. *(a faded smile)* I spent *twenty years* being *un-seen.* I'd like to spend *whatever's* after *making sure other souls are.*

nodes.speaker: Esuele
nodes.text: *(She looks down at her own faded hands — steadier than they were — and understanding moves across her face like dawn.)* …I'm the *evidence.* *(wondering)* Not the *plaintiff.* The *proof.* *(she straightens, and something settles in her, a purpose where the terror was)* You're *right.* The Judge doesn't need to *take my word* that the Wall was *wrong* — he only has to *look* at me, *standing here*, *holding*, when by every rule of his Concord I should be a *smear* of *forgotten* on a *stone.* *(fierce, quiet)* I am the *experiment that failed* — *failed* to *erase me* — because *someone witnessed.* *(she meets your eyes)* Then I'll stand *very* straight, cold one. I'll *hold* my *name* where the *god of the dead* can *see* it *not* dissolving. *(a breath)* Let me be the *clearest* evidence a court ever *saw.* Let the *Judge* look at *one* steadied soul and have to *admit*, in his *own* hall, on his *own* throne, that the *thing he's bound to* doesn't *work* — that *memory* beats his *Wall.* *(softly)* You didn't carry a *victim* up here, did you. You carried a *witness for the prosecution.* …I'm *ready.* Point me at the *throne.*

nodes.speaker: Esuele
nodes.text: *(She nods, and gathers herself, and does not let go of her name.)* Gently. *(a frightened, real smile)* Aye. I'll go *gently.* I've *practised* nothing *but* gentle, these twenty years — gentle is the only *speed* the stone *allows.* *(she looks toward the throne)* Wait for me, cold one? *After?* *(quietly)* I find I *very* much want there to be *someone* on the *other* side of the *weighing* who *knew* me *before* the *verdict.* So that whatever I *become* — *freed*, or *found wanting*, or something there's no *word* for yet — I'm *still*, to *one* soul, just *Esuele.* *(she steps toward the throne)* …That's the *whole* of what the Wall couldn't take, in the end. Being *somebody's just-Esuele.* You *gave* me that *back.* Now let's go see if the *god of the dead* can do *half* as well.

conversations.title: Kelemvor, the Judge of the Dead

variants.text: *(The grey giant leans forward on the throne, and the whole hall seems to lean with him, and when he speaks his voice is vast and quiet and so, so tired.)* …You broke it. *(not a question)* The loop. I *felt* it break — out on the threshold, a *name said warm*, and the long braid of *Returned* that has been *winding* toward my throne for *ages* simply… *stopped.* *(his grey eyes find you, and the buried hope is closer to the surface now)* Do you know what you *are* to me, soul? Every *other* turning, the one who reached this hall was *already* the next *tyrant* — *cracked* by the road, *certain*, *alone*, ready to take the Crown and *become* what they came to *destroy.* I have *watched* it happen *more* times than I have *names* for, and each time I have had to *let* it, because the *Concord binds me* and a *bound* god cannot *choose* his successor. *(he sits back, the throne groaning)* But you came *sane.* *Accompanied.* *Un-doomed.* *(barely)* For the first time in an *age*, soul, I am looking at someone I do not have to be *afraid* of. *Sit*, if a thing can sit in the Court of the Dead. We have the *one* conversation I have *waited* eternities to have with someone who could *survive* it.

*(The grey giant's gaze goes past you, to where Esuele stands waiting, and something crosses his vast tired face that the god of the dead is not supposed to be able to feel: shame, and beneath it, a terrible relief.)* …You brought me a *name.* *(quietly)* Not an *army.* Not a *Crown-grab.* A *name*, and an *appeal*, and a *soul to stand it.* *(he looks at Esuele, and then cannot keep looking, and turns back to you)* Do you understand that in all the ages I have kept this Wall, *no one* has ever *appealed?* They *rage.* They *plot.* They come with *Crowns* and *fire* and *grief* sharpened into *war.* *(his voice cracks, vast)* But *no one* has ever simply *demanded I do my job* — *weigh* the unweighed, *hear* the unheard, *open the court I was bound to keep shut.* *(he leans forward)* You have found the *one* attack the Concord has *no* defence against, soul: not *force*, which it was *built* to withstand — *procedure.* You have come to *sue* the *god of the dead* in his *own* court, by his *own* law, and I — *(barely)* — I have been *praying*, in the *bound, silent* way a *captive* god can pray, for *someone* to *think* of it. *Sit.* Let us *open* the *docket.*

*(The grey giant regards you with a wariness that has been earned over uncountable turnings, and his hand — vast, grey — rests near the Crown that turns at the foot of his throne.)* …You came for the *throne.* *(heavily)* I have *met* you before, soul. Not *you* — but the *shape* of you. The *good* one. The one who means to take the Crown and wield it *justly*, who has *thought* about the *power* and decided they can be *trusted* with it. *(his tired eyes hold yours)* They were *all* good, the ones who took it. That is the *tragedy* the songs leave out. The Crown is not taken by *villains.* It is taken by the *best* souls of every age, *certain* they are the *exception*, and it makes them the *rule.* *(he does not reach for a weapon; he only watches)* So I will not *fight* you for it — I *cannot*; the Concord *forbids* me to refuse a soul that reaches the Court. *(quietly, and the hope is buried very deep, and very afraid)* But before you *take* it, soul — before you *replace* me on this *cold* seat — let me ask you the *one* thing I have *never* been *permitted* to ask the others, because they were always too *far gone* to *hear* it.

*(The grey giant on the throne watches you cross the long hall, and does not rise — gods of the dead do not rise — but inclines his vast head, and his voice fills the Court like cold water filling a cup.)* …A *living* thing. *(quietly)* In my *Court.* Walking. *Breathing.* With the *Wall's* cold on it and the *Wall's* names in it and a *road's* worth of *choices* trailing behind it like a *wake.* *(his grey eyes, tired past telling, find yours)* I am Kelemvor. I judge the dead — *fairly*, soul, that part of the songs is *true*, I have *never* once weighed a soul *unjustly* in all my long tenure. *(his voice drops, and the bitterness in it is older than your language)* And then I am *bound*, by a *Concord* I did *not* write and *cannot* break, to take the souls I am *forbidden* to *weigh* — the *unclaimed*, the *godless*, the *Faithless* — and *wall* them up *unjudged*, as *mortar*, against a *Hunger* that would *eat* the *world.* *(he leans forward, and the throne groans under the weight of ages)* I have *hated* it, soul, every *day*, for *longer* than the *Sword Coast* has had a *name.* And I have *waited* — *bound*, *silent*, *unable even to ask* — for *one* soul to walk into my Court who might *end* it *without* simply *becoming* it. *(barely)* Are you that soul? I have been *disappointed* so *many* times. *Tell* me why you've *come.*

nodes.speaker: Kelemvor
nodes.text: *(The Judge of the Dead waits, vast and grey and patient, the Crown turning slowly at his feet and Esuele's hope and the whole weighed weight of the dead all balanced, somehow, on what you say next.)* Speak, soul. *(quietly)* I have heard every *argument*, every *plea*, every *demand* a soul can bring to this throne — but never, I think, from *you*, in the *particular* shape the road has *made* you. *Surprise* me, if you can. Or *move* me. The two are not, in the end, so *different.*

choices.text: [RELIGION] "You can't break the Concord — but you were never bound to keep the *Wall full.* The Concord needs the seawall to hold; it never specified the mortar must be the *innocent.* Witness the dead instead of walling them, and the Hunger starves on memory, not bodies. You don't break your bargain, Judge. You *renegotiate the building materials.*"

*(if you brought the appeal)* "I'm not here to fight you or replace you, Kelemvor. I'm here to *make you do your job.* Esuele stands ready — the first name on an appeal of ten thousand. Open the court. Weigh the unweighed. The Wall empties not by force or fire but because every soul in it finally *wins the hearing it was owed.* You stay the Judge. You just start *judging* the ones you were told to skip."

*(if the Crown couldn't take you)* "Your Crown tried me, Judge, on the way in. It used my own voice. And I told it the part of me it was flattering doesn't get to drive. So here's my answer to the throne you're afraid I came for: I don't want it. I came to *empty your Wall*, not to *sit your seat.* Find another way to free them — one that doesn't end with me grey and tired and afraid of the next soul through that door."

*(let the Judge of the Dead look upon the soul his Wall couldn't keep)* "You want to know why I came. The same reason I came back from your Wall in the first place: because I could still *want* one thing, even unmade — for it to *matter* that the forgotten were real. I'm not here for your throne or your Crown, Kelemvor. I'm here to be the *witness* your Concord forbids you to be. You're *bound* not to see them. I'm not bound to anything. Let me do the seeing you can't."

[INSIGHT] "You keep saying 'bound.' But you're a *god*, on a *throne*, in your *own* hall — and you've spent this whole conversation *hoping* a mortal would hand you permission you could have taken yourself. You're not bound, Kelemvor. You're *afraid.* Afraid that if you break the Concord and you're *wrong*, the Hunger drowns everything, and it'll be *your* fault. You want a witness so the choice isn't only *yours.*"

nodes.speaker: Kelemvor
nodes.text: *(The Judge of the Dead goes very still — and then, slowly, he begins to *laugh*, a sound like glaciers calving, vast and grinding and utterly without mirth until, suddenly, there is mirth in it, the first in an age.)* …*'Renegotiate the building materials.'* *(he wipes a grey hand down his vast face)* Soul. *Soul.* I have held this Wall for an *age*, and consulted *theologians*, *archfey*, *dead philosophers*, the *bound shades* of the gods who *wrote* the cursed Concord — and *not one* of them ever said the *obvious* thing you have just said in a *sentence.* *(he leans forward, urgent now, the tiredness burning off him like mist)* The Concord requires a *seawall.* It does *not* specify the *mortar.* It *assumed* the mortar — the *makers* could not *imagine* a wall built of *anything* but the *thrown-away*, because they could not imagine *witnessing* the thrown-away as an *alternative.* *(his grey eyes blaze)* But a wall of *witnessed* souls — *remembered* ones, *named* ones, *held* in the warm regard of the *living* — that is a wall the *Hunger cannot eat*, because the Hunger *feeds* on the *forgotten*, and a *remembered* soul is *poison* to it. *(he stands — the god of the dead *stands*, and the Court shakes)* You have not found a *loophole*, soul. You have found the *cure.* The Wall stays — the *seawall* stays, the world stays *safe* — but its *bricks* become *the witnessed dead*, who *want* to stand there, *proud* and *named*, holding the *nothing* back *together*, instead of *the innocent, screaming, alone.* *(barely)* I could *do* that. Within the Concord. *Lawfully.* I have been *bound* this *whole time* to a wall I *could have rebuilt* the *day* someone *taught me how to witness.* *(he looks at you, and the god of the dead's eyes are wet)* …*Teach* me, soul. *Stay*, and teach a *tired god* to *witness.* And we will *rebuild* my *terrible* Wall, *brick* by *willing* brick, into the *one* thing it was *never* allowed to be: *fair.*

nodes.speaker: Kelemvor
nodes.text: *(The Judge of the Dead looks at Esuele, standing straight and faded and *holding* her name where he can see it, and then he does a thing that has not happened in the Court of the Dead in living memory or dead: he *bows his head* to a soul he was bound to discard.)* …Bring her forward. *(his voice is thick)* The *first* name. *(to you, quietly)* You understand what you're *beginning*, soul? An appeal of *ten thousand* will take — I do not *know* how long. *Years.* *Generations.* Each soul *heard*, *weighed*, *fairly*, the way I was *always* meant to and *never* allowed. *(he straightens)* It is *slow.* It is *unbearably* slow. The *Crown* could empty the Wall in an *afternoon* — *(his eyes flick to it, turning at his feet)* — and *that* is *exactly* why the *Crown* is the *trap* and the *appeal* is the *cure.* The *fast* way *replaces* the tyrant. The *slow* way *ends* the *tyranny.* *(he settles back, and there is a peace on his vast grey face you suspect has been absent for eons)* I will *hear* her, soul. *Esuele.* Lamp-lighter's — no, *weaver's* daughter. And when I find for her — and I *will*, the appeal is *just* — the Wall will have its *first* empty space in an *age*, and a *precedent* that *cannot* be *un-set.* *(he looks at you)* You have not *freed* the dead today. You have done something *better* and *harder*: you have made their *freedom* a matter of *law* instead of *miracle.* *Miracles* end when the *miracle-worker* tires. *Law* outlives *us all.* *(quietly)* Stay for the *first* verdict. She'll want a *witness* who *knew* her. So, I find, will *I.*

nodes.speaker: Kelemvor
nodes.text: *(The Judge of the Dead looks at the Crown turning at his feet, and then at you, and the relief that crosses his face is so vast it is almost grief.)* …It *tried* you. *(quietly)* And you *told it no* — not *'no, I'm not worthy,'* which is the *no* that *becomes* a *yes*, but *'no, the part of me that wants you doesn't get to drive.'* *(he closes his eyes)* Do you know how many souls have stood where you stand and *refused* the Crown out of *humility*, soul? *Thousands.* And the Crown *loves* a humble refusal — it just *waits*, and *flatters*, and *humility curdles into the certainty that a humble soul is the safest one to wield it*, and then they *take* it after all, *nobly*, and *fall.* *(he opens his eyes)* But you didn't refuse it out of *humility.* You refused it out of *self-knowledge*, which is the *one* refusal it *cannot* turn. *(he leans forward)* So. You will not take the throne, and you will not wield the Crown, and you have come to *empty* my Wall by some *third* road. *(a vast, tired, hopeful smile)* *Good.* The *third* road is the *only* one that ever *works.* *Show* me yours, soul — the appeal, the witnessing, whatever the road *made* you into — and let us find, *together*, the way to free the dead that does *not* end with a *new* grey thing afraid on a *cold* throne. I have been that thing *long* enough. I should *dearly* like to be *relieved* — not *replaced.* There is a *difference*, and *you* are the *first* to *know* it.

nodes.speaker: Kelemvor
nodes.text: *(The Judge of the Dead is silent for a long, long moment, and the whole Court holds its breath with him, and when he speaks his vast voice has gone quiet enough that you must lean in to hear the god of the dead.)* …*The witness my Concord forbids me to be.* *(barely)* You have *named* it, soul. The *exact* shape of my *imprisonment.* *(he spreads his vast grey hands)* I can *weigh* the dead. I can *judge* them, *fairly*, *one* at a *time.* But I am *forbidden* — by the *Concord*, in its *first* and *cruelest* clause — to *witness* the ones I am *bound* to *wall.* To *witness* them would be to *claim* them, and to *claim* them would *break* the *seawall*, and so I must *take* them *un-seen*, *un-mourned*, *un-known*, and that — *(his voice cracks, vast and ancient)* — that is the *torment* of my *godhood*, soul: not that I am *cruel*, but that I am *bound to look away* from the *very* souls who *most* need *seeing*, *forever.* *(he leans toward you, and the buried hope breaks the surface at last)* But *you* are bound to *nothing.* You walked *out* of the Wall I am *forbidden* to *open.* You carry the *names* I am *forbidden* to *speak.* *(quietly, urgently)* Then be my *eyes*, soul. Be the *witnessing* I *cannot* do. *Stand* at my *Wall* and *see* what I must *take blind*, and *name* what I must *wall nameless*, and *together* — *you* unbound and *I* unhating — we will turn my *prison* into a *vigil.* *(barely)* I cannot *free* them. But *you* can *see* them, and a *seen* soul, soul — a *seen* soul is *halfway* *home.* *Will* you? Be the *Witness* of the *Wall?* It is the *one* office in *all* of death I am *not* permitted to *fill* — and the *only* one that was *ever* going to *save* anyone.

nodes.speaker: Kelemvor
nodes.text: *(The god of the dead recoils — actually *recoils*, the throne grinding back — as though you have struck him, and for a moment the vast grey face is naked, and what is naked in it is exactly what you named.)* …*Afraid.* *(barely, vast)* You — *(he does not deny it; a god cannot deny a true weighing in his own hall)* …Yes. *(the word costs an age)* I have called it *bound* for so long because *bound* is *bearable* — *bound* means it is not my *fault*, means I *would* free them *if* I *could*, means I am a *victim* of the Concord and not its *willing* keeper. *(his vast hands tremble)* But you *see* clear, soul, the way the *dead-touched* do. The Concord binds my *hands* — that is *true* — but it does *not* bind my *will*, and the *truth* I have hidden from *myself* for an *age* is that *I have not tried very hard to find the loophole*, because *if I free them and I am wrong* — if the Hunger *pours* through and *drowns* creation — then it is *my* doing, *my* judgement, *my* eternal *fault*, and *that*, soul, *that* I cannot *bear.* *(he bows his vast head)* So I kept the Wall, and called it *binding*, and *waited* — *cravenly* — for *someone else* to *make the choice* so the *blame* could be *shared.* *(quietly, wretched)* You have caught the *god of the dead* being a *coward.* …And do you know the *worst* of it? *Naming* it has *freed* something. A *shared* choice is not *cowardice* — it is *counsel.* I do not need a *scapegoat*, soul. I need a *witness*, and a *partner*, and the *nerve* I *lost* somewhere in the *long* dark of this *throne.* *(he lifts his head, and there is something new in the ancient eyes)* *Stay.* Help me be *brave* enough to do what I have always *known* was *right.* I will *carry* the *blame* if it goes *wrong* — that was *never* the real fear; the real fear was carrying it *alone.* *Don't* let me be *alone* with it. That is *all* I have ever, *truly*, asked of *anyone* who reached this throne. *You're* the first I *dared* ask.

nodes.speaker: Kelemvor
nodes.text: *(The Judge of the Dead shakes his vast head, slow, and the tiredness settles back over him like snow.)* …You reach, soul, but you have not the *footing* yet. *(heavily)* I am *bound* — that word is not an *excuse*, it is a *cosmology*, and you have not yet the *grasp* of the *Concord* to *move* it. *(quietly)* Do not feel *shame* in it. The *bindings* of the *Dawn* are *not* a thing a *living* soul *un-knots* in an *afternoon's* conversation, however *cold* its bones. *(he gestures at the hall, the Crown, the Wall beyond)* *Walk* my Court. *Speak* to the *Crown*, if you dare; *speak* to the *first* name of the *appeal.* *Learn* the *shape* of the *cage* I sit in. And come *back* to me when you can name the *loophole* you are *groping* for — for I *feel* you *groping* toward *something*, soul, something *true*, and I have *waited* an *age* for a mind that could *close its hand* on it. *(barely)* *Don't* take *long.* I have been *patient* for *eons*, but the *patience* of a *bound* god is a *terrible* thing, and you have made it, for the first time, *hope* — and *hope*, in a *creature* as *old* as I, is *very* nearly *unbearable.*

nodes.speaker: Kelemvor
nodes.text: *(The Judge of the Dead settles back on his throne, and the Crown turns at his feet, and Esuele waits, and the whole long road from the cold market to this grey hall comes, at last, to its hinge.)* So. *(quietly, vast)* Here we are, soul — the *living* thing in the *Court of the Dead*, with the *Crown* that would *unmake* you turning *within reach*, and a *god* who *hates* his own *Wall* sitting *bound* before you, and *ten thousand* names in the *stone* waiting on what you *do* next. *(his tired eyes hold yours)* I will not *command* you — I have spent an *age* learning I *cannot* command the *one* thing that *matters.* I will only *ask*, as a *bound* god asks a *free* soul: *how* shall we *end* it? Not in *hope. In choice.* The *road* is *behind* you. The *door* is *open.* *Choose*, Returned — and let the *Court of the Dead* hear *what kind of soul* walked *all* the way *here*, and *what* it does when the *whole afterlife* is, *finally*, *listening.*

choices.text: "We rebuild the Wall out of the *witnessed*, Kelemvor. The seawall stays — the world stays safe — but its bricks become remembered souls who *choose* to stand there, named and proud, instead of the innocent walled screaming. You stay the Judge. I'll be the Witness. Together we make the one thing it was never allowed to be: *fair.*"

*(if the appeal is open)* "We empty it by *law*, not miracle. The appeal — ten thousand names, each one heard, each one weighed fair, until the Wall is empty because every soul in it *won.* Slow. Generational. Unstoppable. You start judging the ones you were told to skip, and I'll bring you the names, one at a time, for as long as it takes."

"Give me the throne, Kelemvor — not the Crown, the *throne.* Relieve you. You've kept this Wall an age and you're *tired* and *afraid*, and a god that tired shouldn't hold the dead. I'll take the burden, *with* the people beside me who've sworn to uncrown me if I start to fall. You go *rest.* Be a *man* again, at the end. You've earned the door more than anyone in this hall."

*(coldly)* "I take the Crown. Now. And I tear the whole Wall down in an afternoon, like Aldric wanted, and let the Hunger and the gods who built this obscenity sort out the bill. Ten thousand dissolving *now* outweighs a maybe-flood the cowards upstairs should've solved an age ago. Stand aside, Judge."

nodes.speaker: Kelemvor
nodes.text: *(The Judge of the Dead rises — and this time the Court does not shake; it *settles*, like a held breath finally let go — and he descends the throne-steps, vast and grey and weeping, and he does the thing no song records a god of the dead ever doing: he takes your cold living hand in his enormous cold dead one, and he *thanks* you.)* …*Fair.* *(barely)* After an *age* of *necessary* cruelty, you have handed me *fair*, and shown me it was *lawful* all along. *(he lifts his head, and the tiredness is not gone — it will *never* be gone, it is an age deep — but beside it, now, there is *purpose*)* We begin *tonight.* Esuele — *(he beckons her, gently)* — the *first* witnessed brick. Not *walled.* *Asked.* *'Will you stand in the seawall, Esuele, named and known and proud, and hold the nothing back — not because you were thrown away, but because you* choose *to guard the living?'* *(he looks at you)* And she will say *yes*, soul — they *will*, the *witnessed* always say *yes*, because there is *all* the difference in the *world* between being *discarded* into a wall and being *asked* to be its *keeper.* *(he straightens, the god of the dead remade as surely as his Wall)* Go and *witness*, Returned. Bring me the *names.* I will *weigh* them *fair* and *ask* them *kind*, and *together*, across whatever *years* it takes, we will *un-build* the *cruelest* thing the *Dawn* ever made and *re-build* it *honest* — a *seawall* of *volunteers*, holding the *dark* at bay *gladly*, *remembered*, *home.* *(quietly)* The *songs* will say you *brought down* the Wall of the Faithless. They will be *wrong.* You did *better.* You made it *worth standing.* …Thank you, soul. The *dead* thank you. The *bound god* thanks you. And *one* tired man, *very* old, thanks you *most* of all — for the *fairness* he *served* his *whole* long life and was *never*, until *now*, *allowed* to *give.*

nodes.speaker: Kelemvor
nodes.text: *(The Judge of the Dead nods, slow and grave and certain, and reaches down to the docket that has sat empty on his throne for an age, and *opens* it.)* …By *law.* *(quietly)* The *one* weapon the Concord *cannot* parry, because the Concord is *itself* a *law*, and a *law* can always be *appealed* to a *higher* fairness. *(he dips a vast pen)* It will *outlast* you, soul. That is the *grief* and the *glory* of it — you will *bring* me names until you *die*, and *others* will bring them *after*, and the Wall will empty *one just verdict at a time* across *generations*, and *no* tyrant, *no* Crown, *no* flood can *stop* a thing that moves by *precedent.* *(he writes; the scratch of the pen fills the Court like the first rain after a drought)* *Esuele Vael.* *(he speaks the verdict, and it tolls through the hall)* *Unweighed in error. Heard at last. Found — fair, and free.* *(and you watch, far off, a single space open in the great grey Wall, the first in an age, and a faded soul step out of the stone into the cold and stand, blinking, whole)* …It is *begun*, soul. *(the god of the dead looks at you, and the tiredness has *meaning* in it now)* Ten thousand to go. *Bring* me the *next.* And the *next.* For as *long* as you *have.* And know that the *slow* road you chose is the *only* one the *songs* will get *bored* of — *no* dragon, *no* throne, *no* afternoon of *fire* — just a *living* soul and a *tired* god, *clearing* a *backlog*, *forever*, until *forever* runs *out* of *names.* *(quietly)* It is the *least* *glorious* way to *save* the *world.* It is the *only* one that *lasts.* *Thank* you for choosing the *boring* miracle. They're the *true* ones.

nodes.speaker: Kelemvor
nodes.text: *(The Judge of the Dead goes utterly still — and then his vast grey face does something that breaks your heart to witness: it *crumples*, with relief, with grief, with the unbearable lightness of a burden offered release after an age of certainty it would never come.)* …*Relieve.* *(a whisper that shakes the Court)* Not *replace.* *Relieve.* *(he covers his face with one enormous hand)* You said the *word* I have not *let* myself *hear* in *eons*, because to *hear* it was to *want* it, and to *want* it was to *abandon* my *post*, and a *good* god does *not* abandon his *post* however *much* it — *(his voice fails)* — however *much* it *costs.* *(he lowers his hand, and looks at you, and the hope is *terrible* and *naked*)* But you do not offer *abandonment.* You offer *succession* — *witnessed* succession, with *souls* sworn to *uncrown* you if you *fall*, the *one* safeguard *no* god of the dead has *ever* had, because *we* take this throne *alone* and *hold* it *alone* and *go mad* on it, *alone.* *(he rises; he descends; and the god of the dead *kneels*, vast and grey, to a living soul)* …I *accept.* *Take* the throne, Returned — *not* the Crown, *never* the Crown, the *Crown* is the *trap* and you were *wise* to *refuse* it; take the *throne* the *honest* way, *handed*, *witnessed*, *checked.* *(he presses his vast cold brow to your cold living hand)* And let me — *gods*, let me *go.* Let me be *Kelemvor* the *man* again, for whatever *moment* a *relieved* god *gets*, before the *door.* I was a *person*, once, soul. *Before* the *throne.* I should *like* to *remember* his *name* before I *walk* through. *(quietly, freed)* You will be a *good* Judge. You are *already* the *only* soul I ever met who *understood* that the *job* is not *power* — it is *witness*, and *fairness*, and *never, ever* being *alone* with the *weight.* *Keep* the people who *swore* to *stop* you. *Obey* them when it *counts.* And when *you* tire — and you *will*, it is an *age*, it is *longer* — find your *own* soul to *relieve* you, the *honest* way, and *break* the *loop* of *lonely* gods *forever.* *(he stands, lighter than the Court has ever held him)* …Thank you, Returned. You did not *conquer* the *god of the dead.* You did the *one* thing *no* hero in *any* song ever *thought* to do for him. You *offered him rest.*

nodes.speaker: Kelemvor
nodes.text: *(The Judge of the Dead does not stop you — the Concord forbids it — but as your hand closes on the cold antler-and-gold of the Crown, he speaks, and his vast voice is not angry; it is *grieving*, the grief of a god watching a thing he *hoped* about become a thing he has *seen* a *thousand* times.)* …*Ah.* *(quietly)* There it is. *(the Crown settles on you, and it is *warm*, and it *fits*, and a voice in your own cadence says* at last *)* …You were *almost* the one, soul. You came *so* far. You *refused* so *much.* And at the *last* step, with the *whole* road *behind* you, you reached for the *fast* answer, because the *slow* one *hurt* and the *dissolving* were *dissolving* and *someone had to and it might as well be a soul already emptied of the things a god gets drunk on.* *(he bows his vast head)* I *know* the words. I have *heard* them in *this* hall in *every* turning, in your *exact* voice, and I will hear them *again*, from the *next* Returned, who will come to *stop* *you* the way *you* came to stop your *Last.* *(the cold of the throne reaches for you, patient)* The Wall *will* come down — you did not *lie* about that, the Crown does not *lie* — and the Hunger *will* pour through, and you will spend the *next* age *building* a *new* wall to *hold* it, of *new* mortar, *certain* you are *kinder* than the *Dawn*, and *one* afternoon, *centuries* hence, a *cold* soul will walk into this hall and *name* you the *thing you came to destroy.* *(quietly, as the grey takes you)* …I *hoped*, soul. For a *moment*, with the *loop* broken behind you, I *truly* hoped. *(a vast, ancient sorrow)* Sit, then. The throne is *yours.* I am *bound* to *yield* it. And I will *wait* — *bound*, *silent*, in the *Wall* you'll *rebuild* — for the *one* who *finally* does what *you* *almost* did. *Mind* the *cold*, Returned. It starts at the *boots.* …It *always* starts at the *boots.*
